channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Supreme Court will weigh Mexico's $10 billion lawsuit against U.S. gun makers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court said Friday it will decide whether to block a $10 billion lawsuit Mexico filed against leading U.S. gun manufacturers over allegations their commercial practices have helped caused much bloodshed there.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor, Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: Teresa De Miguel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh a Texas death row case after halting execution
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court will hear the case of a Texas man on death row who has long argued that DNA testing would help prove he didn't kill an 85-year-old woman during a home robbery decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk to join Trump at rally at the site of first assassination attempt
item.description: Elon Musk will join Donald Trump at his rally Saturday in Butler, the Pennsylvania city where the Republican presidential nominee survived an assassination attempt earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Need a passport? You can now get one faster
item.description: U.S. passport processing time has been reduced by two weeks at most, the State Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Helene’s powerful storm surge killed 12 near Tampa. They didn't have to die
item.description: Hurricane Helene’s storm surge struck Florida fast and hard when it slammed into Gulf Coast communities near St. Petersburg.
item.dc:creator: David Fischer And Terry Spencer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas Blue Alert issued for suspect linked to Memphis officer shooting
item.description: A Blue Alert has been issued in Texas for Seth Altman, 33, who is wanted in connection with the shooting of a law enforcement officer in Memphis, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, KPRC 2

item.title: Serial killers hide as long-haul truckers to terrorize America’s highways, expert says
item.description: For years the FBI investigated highway serial killers who were hiding as long-haul truckers to prey on victims along America's interstates.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother are youngest-known Helene victims
item.description: A large tree had crashed through the roof, crushing Kobe Williams and causing her to fall on top of infant sons Khyzier and Khazmir. All three were found dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Menendez brothers trial juror: 'It was a battle of the sexes'
item.description: "The men did not believe that Jose (Menendez) had been abusing his sons," Thornton said Thursday on "Banfield.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 14 dogs from South Carolina arrive in Rhode Island after Hurricane Helene
item.description: "We are hoping that these dogs get adopted quickly so that we have some space so we can call them again and try to help them some more because they are dealing with so much," Potter League CEO Brad Shear said.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Rhode Island woman works 350 hours to build first home
item.description: "It makes you excited to think that you're going to build your own home and help others to get theirs," she told NewsNatoin affiliate WPRI. "That's like a dream come true.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15 to allow time to negotiate new contract
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend their strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract, a person briefed on the matter says.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Blue Ridge Parkway to be closed indefinitely, National Park Service says
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, NewsNation

item.title: Former Colorado county clerk Tina Peters sentenced to 9 years for voting data scheme
item.description: A judge ripped into a Colorado county clerk for her crimes and lies before sentencing her Thursday to nine years behind bars for a data-breach scheme spawned from the rampant false claims about voting machine fraud in the 2020 presidential race.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania US Senate: Bob Casey, Dave McCormick trade jabs in debate
item.description: The candidates battled throughout the debate, often claiming the other was lying on the campaign trail and on the debate stage.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's student loan cancellation is put on hold again after day of legal whiplash
item.description: A federal judge in Missouri put a temporary hold on President Joe Biden’s latest student loan cancellation plan on Thursday, slamming the door on hope it would move forward after another judge allowed a pause to expire.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: University of Michigan Jewish students, rabbi held at gunpoint during dinner
item.description: A rabbi and a group of Jewish students were held at gunpoint during a dinner late Wednesday, according to the University of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue Ridge Parkway to be closed indefinitely, National Park Service says
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arizona man sentenced after setting fire to Grand Canyon National Park cabin where ex-girlfriend stayed
item.description: A man who set fire to a cabin inside the Grand Canyon National Park was sentenced to 20 months in federal prison, authorities said this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: The ruling came one day before the temporary restraining order would expire, allowing the administration to continue the program while the case is moved to Missouri.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Federal agencies warn of scams in wake of Helene
item.description: “These criminals take advantage of victims before, during, and after a natural disaster strikes, targeting people when they are most vulnerable, and their methods to commit fraud are wide-ranging,” one U.S. Attorney said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: President Joe Biden’s student debt relief plan was dealt a win by a Georgia judge this week by removing one Republican challenger and letting the program advance for now after a temporarily restraining order expires.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Bipartisan California lawmakers urge House leaders to boost disaster relief funding
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers from Orange County, Calif., are urging House leaders to include disaster relief funding in fiscal 2025 spending, highlighting wildfire damage in the state even as damage from Hurricane Helene on the other side of the country has heightened attention on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Frankie Valli addresses fan concerns about elder abuse
item.description: Valli still has a full slate of upcoming shows that will see him crisscrossing the nation, including five in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: Helene waves and storm surge crash into Florida home as owners watch
item.description: Terrifying moments caught on video showed Helene’s powerful and deadly storm surge as it crashed into a home.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, NewsNation

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, Fox 5

item.title: How you can help after Helene
item.description: Reporters who rode out the storm will explain what Americans faced in the hours after Helene arrived. Red Cross representatives will also explain how your donations are being used to help Americans across 10 states get back on their feet.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KRON4

item.title: Meet Spot, the LAPD's new crimefighting robot dog
item.description: The futuristic four-legged machine, which boasts the size and agility of a 70-pound golden retriever, has been met with both fascination and concern.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: South Carolina grandparents killed by fallen tree during Helene are found hugging
item.description: A South Carolina couple were found hugging each other after a tree fell on their bedroom and killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dayton told to 'ready' for evacuation as massive Elk Fire spreads 32,000 acres
item.description: The town of Dayton, Wyo. has been put on a "ready to evacuate" status as a massive wildfire continues to spread across the Bighorn National Forest just six miles away.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Coach praises Tim Walz's son for helping protect other kids after shooting
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — When the issue of gun violence came up at the vice presidential debate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz had a personal story to tell: His teenage son, Gus, had been at a sports center last year when a shooting took place.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A judge considers whether to clarify 2 new Georgia election certification rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge is considering whether to issue clarification or guidance for two new rules from the Georgia State Election Board that have to do with county certification of election results.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
